title: Strict OOF stacking plus post-hoc calibration instead of raw averaging
summary: Different families should capture different structure here: CatBoost for categoricals, GBDT/AFT for event timing, and ranking models for pit-window ordering.
rationale: Different families should capture different structure here: CatBoost for categoricals, GBDT/AFT for event timing, and ranking models for pit-window ordering. Stacking and calibration only help if all meta-features are generated out-of-fold with the same group-aware splits; scikit-learn explicitly warns calibration must use data independent from base-model fitting.
implementation_hint: Generate group-safe OOF predictions from 3-4 diverse models, stack them with a light logistic/meta-GBM using `StackingClassifier`-style logic or manual OOF blending, then calibrate the final score with sigmoid or isotonic on held-out OOF predictions only. Compare raw mean, rank-mean, and calibrated stack.
expected_effect: Often yields a modest but robust AUC gain by combining complementary inductive biases and fixing overconfident tails near pit windows.
risk: Easy to accidentally leak if base and meta layers use inconsistent folds; isotonic can overfit if applied on too little validation data.
